prompt: Create a Rust project skeleton for an ironclad proc-macro crate. Write the following files: status/ironclad/Cargo.toml (proc-macro crate depending on syn quote proc-macro2), status/ironclad/src/lib.rs (empty proc_macro functions for game_value game_lifecycle game_entity), status/ironclad/src/game_value.rs (stub that parses min/max attributes and generates a newtype with a new() constructor that panics on out-of-bounds), status/ironclad/README.md explaining the three macros. Keep it compilable but minimal.

Read src/shared/mod.rs completely. List every struct and enum. For each one identify: which fields could benefit from bounded values (game_value), which enums represent lifecycles or state machines (lifecycle), which structs have required fields that workers might forget (game_entity). Write analysis to status/ironclad-retrofit-plan.md.
